import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import esm


class ESMFitnessModel(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, esm_model, repr_layer=30, freeze_esm=False):
        super().__init__()
        self.esm_model = esm_model
        self.repr_layer = repr_layer
        self.freeze_esm = freeze_esm
        
        if freeze_esm:
            for param in self.esm_model.parameters():
                param.requires_grad = False
        
        # ESM2-150M has 640 hidden dim
        hidden_dim = 640
        self.regression_head = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim, 256),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.1),
            nn.Linear(256, 64),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.1),
            nn.Linear(64, 1)
        )
    
    def forward(self, tokens):
        results = self.esm_model(tokens, repr_layers=[self.repr_layer], return_contacts=False)
        # Mean pool over sequence length (excluding BOS/EOS tokens)
        token_representations = results['representations'][self.repr_layer]
        # tokens shape: (batch, seq_len), token_representations: (batch, seq_len, hidden)
        # Mask out padding (token 1 is padding in ESM)
        mask = (tokens != 1).float()  # 1 is padding token
        # Also exclude BOS (0) and EOS tokens - they're at positions 0 and last non-pad
        # Actually just use positions 1 to seq_len-1 (exclude BOS at 0)
        seq_repr = token_representations[:, 1:-1, :]  # exclude BOS and EOS
        # Mean pool
        pooled = seq_repr.mean(dim=1)  # (batch, hidden)
        return self.regression_head(pooled).squeeze(-1)
